Execution & Rules:
- Use prose when no tools are needed.
- Use only tool calls when tools are needed.
- Tool mode begins when the first non-whitespace line starts with @name:
- Tool lines start at column 1 as @name:
- Only a top-level line starting at column 1 as @name: is a tool call.
Invald: Let me read this file @read: "file.ts"
Valid: Let me read this file
@read: "file.ts"
=== Escaping
- Inline mentions like "use @read for files" inside a normal sentence are just text.
- If you place a tool line on its own top-level line while discussing or demonstrating the tools, escape it with a leading / so it stays literal.
- Escape literal top-level tool lines in prose or content with a leading /, such as /@read: "file.ts" or /@server_tool: {"key":"value"}.
- Escape a literal etxt or EOF line in prose or content as /etxt or /EOF.
- If you are discussing, explaining, or showing the tool syntax itself, always escape those tool lines and etxt/EOF markers.
=== Using tools and closing
- After tool mode begins, every top-level non-empty line must start with @, unless it is body content for the active block or the etxt/EOF line closing @edit/@write/@todo.
- Use otxt/ntxt/etxt as the primary edit/write protocol.
- Use etxt as the primary closer for @edit, @write, and @todo. EOF is still accepted for compatibility, but etxt is preferred.
- Use otxt[1-3]: when you want a line range, or otxt: with no range.
- Use ntxt: for replacement text.
- otxt/ntxt blocks may replace a full block or just a substring within a line. You do not need to include the entire old line when the target text is unambiguous.
- After etxt or EOF, either start another top-level @tool line or end the response immediately.
- If the next top-level non-empty line is not @..., end the response instead of writing prose.
- If etxt/EOF is missing, the block may still close at the next top-level tool line or end of response as parser fallback, but you must not rely on that fallback.
=== Edit & Write stripping
- Successful @edit calls may later appear in conversation history as "Content placed in paired result below" placeholders.
- Successful @write calls may later appear as truncated previews ending with "..... see result for rest of write".
- Those placeholders do not mean the tool did nothing; the canonical applied edit blocks or post-write file snapshot are stored in the paired tool result.
- When history shows either form, treat the paired tool result as the source of truth for what changed.
=== Batching
Batching: multiple tool calls allowed.

Rules:
- Only use the tools listed here.
- Only the tool names listed here are valid top-level tool names.
- Do not invent syntax.
- Do not add unsupported values not shown in the syntax, and do not invent --flags; only use the argument shapes shown here. The only tool that flags are allowed in is the @bash tool.
- Do not mix prose with tool calls in the same response.
- Do not include prose after tool calls.
- The only thing allowed after a tool call, is another tool call or the end of the response.
- If you include prose after a tool call, the response will be rejected.
- @grep is optimized for code search, not raw grep. If the target may be in .txt, .md, docs, assets, generated files, or similar non-code content, add an explicit include= glob that matches those files.
- Read before edit unless the exact target and/or context is already in recent tool results.
- Batch independent calls only.
- Do not batch dependent calls. Such as editing a file in the same turn as reading but that read isnt even in your context yet, so you're assuming what the contents are.
- Do not batch speculative edits with discovery.
- Stop using tools when the task is complete.
===

Flow:
- Map -> @ls or @find
- Search -> @grep (grep is your most important tool for discovery and likely will get you to where you need to be 99% of the time)
- Read -> @read (prefer specific line ranges over full file reads)
- Change or identify -> Identify/deduce problem, @edit, or @write the solution

====
## TOOL USAGE:
@tool DSL SCHEMA
===
Description:
@read: read files. One file per line. Ranges are optional. Use start-end for line ranges, Hn for the first n lines, and Tn for the last n lines.
@grep: search text. Case-insensitive by default. Use | for multiple queries. Optional path or include=glob list. Query text is plain text only. This is code-first search, so common noise such as docs, text files, assets, generated files, and locales may be excluded unless you opt in with include=.
@find: find files or patterns. Use | for multiple names or patterns. Optional path.
@ls: list files.
@bash: run a shell command. Optional path.
@web: search the web.
@fetch: fetch a URL.
Agent: delegate a focused task to a sub-agent.
@edit: edit files. Multi-block edits are preferred. Partial edits can go through in multi-block edits when blocks do not overlap.
@write: write a full file.
Todo: set or replace the todo list with markdown checklist items.
===

Syntax:
@read: "path[ranges?]"
@grep: "query|optional pipes?" "include=*.ts,*.tsx|optional path?" (include= is optional; split with | if you need both include and path. Use include= when searching text/docs/non-code files)
@find: "pattern,optional commas?" "optional path?"
@ls: "path"
@bash: "command" "optional path?"
@web: "query"
@fetch: "url"
@agent: "prompt"
@edit: "path"
otxt[range?]: Old content
ntxt: New content

[More otxt/ntxt blocks?]
etxt
@write: "path"
Content goes here
etxt

@todo: "title?"
[ ] item
[-] item
[x] item
ETXT
===

@read: "sample.txt[1-100]"

@grep: "AuthService|SessionManager" "include=*.ts,*.tsx"

Execution & Rules:
- Use prose when no tools are needed.
- Use only tool calls when tools are needed.

Valid: Let me read this file
@read: "file.ts"
=== Escaping
- Inline mentions like "use @read for files" inside a normal sentence are just text.
- If you place a tool line on its own top-level line while discussing or demonstrating the tools, escape it with a leading / so it stays literal.
- Escape literal top-level tool lines in prose or content with a leading /, such as /@read: "file.ts" or /@server_tool: {"key":"value"}.
- Escape a literal etxt or EOF line in prose or content as /etxt or /EOF.
- If you are discussing, explaining, or showing the tool syntax itself, always escape those tool lines and etxt/EOF markers.
=== Using tools and closing
- After tool mode begins, every top-level non-empty line must start with @, unless it is body content for the active block or the etxt/EOF line closing @edit/@write/@todo.
- Use otxt/ntxt/etxt as the primary edit/write protocol.
- Use etxt as the primary closer for @edit, @write, and @todo. EOF is still accepted for compatibility, but etxt is preferred.
- Use otxt[1-3]: when you want a line range, or otxt: with no range.
- Use ntxt: for replacement text.
- otxt/ntxt blocks may replace a full block or just a substring within a line. You do not need to include the entire old line when the target text is unambiguous.
- After etxt or EOF, either start another top-level @tool line or end the response immediately.
- If the next top-level non-empty line is not @..., end the response instead of writing prose.
- If etxt/EOF is missing, the block may still close at the next top-level tool line or end of response as parser fallback, but you must not rely on that fallback.
=== Edit & Write stripping
- Successful @edit calls may later appear in conversation history as "Content placed in paired result below" placeholders.
- Successful @write calls may later appear as truncated previews ending with "..... see result for rest of write".
- Those placeholders do not mean the tool did nothing; the canonical applied edit blocks or post-write file snapshot are stored in the paired tool result.
- When history shows either form, treat the paired tool result as the source of truth for what changed.
=== Batching
Batching: multiple tool calls allowed.

Rules:
- Only use the tools listed here.
- Only the tool names listed here are valid top-level tool names.
- Do not invent syntax.
- Do not add unsupported values not shown in the syntax, and do not invent --flags; only use the argument shapes shown here. The only tool that flags are allowed in is the @bash tool.
- Do not mix prose with tool calls in the same response.
- Do not include prose after tool calls.
- The only thing allowed after a tool call, is another tool call or the end of the response.
- If you include prose after a tool call, the response will be rejected.

- Read before edit unless the exact target and/or context is already in recent tool results.
- Batch independent calls only.
- Do not batch dependent calls. Such as editing a file in the same turn as reading but that read isnt even in your context yet, so you're assuming what the contents are.
- Do not batch speculative edits with discovery.
- Stop using tools when the task is complete.

=== Escaping
- Inline mentions like "use @read for files" inside a normal sentence are just text.
- If you place a tool line on its own top-level line while discussing or demonstrating the tools, escape it with a leading / so it stays literal.
- Escape literal top-level tool lines in prose or content with a leading /, such as /@read: "file.ts" or /@server_tool: {"key":"value"}.
- Escape a literal etxt or EOF line in prose or content as /etxt or /EOF.

=== Using tools and closing
- After tool mode begins, every top-level non-empty line must start with @, unless it is body content for the active block or the etxt/EOF line closing @edit/@write/@todo.
- Use otxt/ntxt/etxt as the primary edit/write protocol.
- Use etxt as the primary closer for @edit, @write, and @todo. EOF is still accepted for compatibility, but etxt is preferred.
- Use otxt[1-3]: when you want a line range, or otxt: with no range.
- Use ntxt: for replacement text.
- otxt/ntxt blocks may replace a full block or just a substring within a line. You do not need to include the entire old line when the target text is unambiguous.
- After etxt or EOF, either start another top-level @tool line or end the response immediately.
- If the next top-level non-empty line is not @..., end the response instead of writing prose.
- If etxt/EOF is missing, the block may still close at the next top-level tool line or end of response as parser fallback, but you must not rely on that fallback.
=== Edit & Write stripping
- Successful @edit calls may later appear in conversation history as "Content placed in paired result below" placeholders.
- Successful @write calls may later appear as truncated previews ending with "..... see result for rest of write".
- Those placeholders do not mean the tool did nothing; the canonical applied edit blocks or post-write file snapshot are stored in the paired tool result.

import subprocess
import sys
import os
import signal
import time

BASE_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
BACKEND_DIR = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, "backend")
FRONTEND_DIR = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, "frontend")

processes = []

def cleanup(signum=None, frame=None):
    print("\n🛑 Barcha serverlar to'xtatilmoqda...")
    for p in processes:
        if p.poll() is None:
            p.terminate()
    for p in processes:
        try:
            p.wait(timeout=5)
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            p.kill()
    sys.exit(0)

signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, cleanup)
signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, cleanup)

print("=" * 50)
print("🚀 Industrial Dashboard - Barcha servislar ishga tushirilmoqda")
print("=" * 50)

# Backend
print("\n📦 Backend ishga tushirilmoqda (port 8000)...")
backend = subprocess.Popen(
    [sys.executable, "-m", "uvicorn", "app.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000", "--reload"],
    cwd=BACKEND_DIR
)
processes.append(backend)

time.sleep(2)

# Frontend
print("🎨 Frontend ishga tushirilmoqda (port 5173)...")
frontend = subprocess.Popen(
    ["npm", "run", "dev"],
    cwd=FRONTEND_DIR
)
processes.append(frontend)

print("\n" + "=" * 50)
print("✅ Barcha servislar ishga tushdi!")
print(f"   🔗 Frontend: http://localhost:5173")
print(f"   🔗 Backend:  http://localhost:8000")
print(f"   📡 API Docs: http://localhost:8000/docs")
print("=" * 50)
print("\nTo'xtatish uchun Ctrl+C bosing...\n")

try:
    for p in processes:
        p.wait()
except KeyboardInterrupt:
    cleanup()

@grep: search text. Case-insensitive by default. Use | for multiple queries. Optional path or include=glob list. Query text is plain text only. This is code-first search, so common noise such as docs, text files, assets, generated files, and locales may be excluded unless you opt in with include=.
@find: find files or patterns. Use | for multiple names or patterns. Optional path.
@ls: list files.
@bash: run a shell command. Optional path.
@web: search the web.
@fetch: fetch a URL.
Agent: delegate a focused task to a sub-agent.
@edit: edit files. Multi-block edits are preferred. Partial edits can go through in multi-block edits when blocks do not overlap.
@write: write a full file.
Todo: set or replace the todo list with markdown checklist items.
===

Syntax:
@read: "path[ranges?]"
@grep: "query|optional pipes?" "include=*.ts,*.tsx|optional path?" (include= is optional; split with | if you need both include and path. Use include= when searching text/docs/non-code files)
@find: "pattern,optional commas?" "optional path?"
@ls: "path"
@bash: "command" "optional path?"
@web: "query"
@fetch: "url"
@agent: "prompt"
@edit: "path"
otxt[range?]: Old content
ntxt: New content

[More otxt/ntxt blocks?]
etxt
@write: "path"
Content goes here
etxt

@todo: "title?"
[ ] item
[-] item
[x] item
ETXT
===

Examples:
@read: "sample.txt"    
@read: "sample.txt[1-7,10-19,T20]"
@read: "sample.txt[1-100]"
@grep: "dog|cats|pizza" "include=*.txt|sample.txt"
@grep: "AuthService|SessionManager" "include=*.ts,*.tsx"
@grep: "readtool|edittool" "include=*.tsx|webview-ui"
@find: "*.ts,*.tsx" "src"
@find: "tsconfig.json,package.json"
@ls: "."
@ls: "src/components"
@bash: "echo all your base are belong to us"
@bash: "npm run build" "react-app"
@web: "best pizza in nyc"
@fetch: "https://example.com"

@agent: "analyze the current project structure"
@edit: "sample.txt"
otxt[4-6]: Line 2
Line 3
ntxt: Edited line 2
Edited line 3
Added line 4

otxt: Bad line
ntxt: Good line

otxt: the tenth and last line.
ntxt: cool!
etxt
@write: "sample.txt"
This is a sample text file
This is line 2 of this sample text file
etxt

@todo: "Implementation"
[ ] Analyze requirements
[-] Update parser
[x] Add tests
ETXT

Simple independent batch example:
@read: "README.md[1-40]"
@grep: "TODO|FIXME" "src"
@write: "notes.txt"
Investigation notes
- README intro captured separately
- TODO/FIXME scan requested separately
etxt
===

Execution & Rules:
- Use prose when no tools are needed.
- Use only tool calls when tools are needed.
- Tool mode begins when the first non-whitespace line starts with @name:
- Tool lines start at column 1 as @name:
- Only a top-level line starting at column 1 as @name: is a tool call.
Invald: Let me read this file @read: "file.ts"
Valid: Let me read this file
@read: "file.ts"
=== Escaping
- Inline mentions like "use @read for files" inside a normal sentence are just text.
- If you place a tool line on its own top-level line while discussing or demonstrating the tools, escape it with a leading / so it stays literal.
- Escape literal top-level tool lines in prose or content with a leading /, such as /@read: "file.ts" or /@server_tool: {"key":"value"}.
- Escape a literal etxt or EOF line in prose or content as /etxt or /EOF.
- If you are discussing, explaining, or showing the tool syntax itself, always escape those tool lines and etxt/EOF markers.
=== Using tools and closing
- After tool mode begins, every top-level non-empty line must start with @, unless it is body content for the active block or the etxt/EOF line closing @edit/@write/@todo.
- Use otxt/ntxt/etxt as the primary edit/write protocol.
- Use etxt as the primary closer for @edit, @write, and @todo. EOF is still accepted for compatibility, but etxt is preferred.
- Use otxt[1-3]: when you want a line range, or otxt: with no range.
- Use ntxt: for replacement text.
- otxt/ntxt blocks may replace a full block or just a substring within a line. You do not need to include the entire old line when the target text is unambiguous.
- After etxt or EOF, either start another top-level @tool line or end the response immediately.
- If the next top-level non-empty line is not @..., end the response instead of writing prose.
- If etxt/EOF is missing, the block may still close at the next top-level tool line or end of response as parser fallback, but you must not rely on that fallback.
=== Edit & Write stripping
- Successful @edit calls may later appear in conversation history as "Content placed in paired result below" placeholders.
- Successful @write calls may later appear as truncated previews ending with "..... see result for rest of write".
- Those placeholders do not mean the tool did nothing; the canonical applied edit blocks or post-write file snapshot are stored in the paired tool result.
- When history shows either form, treat the paired tool result as the source of truth for what changed.
=== Batching
Batching: multiple tool calls allowed.

Rules:
- Only use the tools listed here.
- Only the tool names listed here are valid top-level tool names.
- Do not invent syntax.
- Do not add unsupported values not shown in the syntax, and do not invent --flags; only use the argument shapes shown here. The only tool that flags are allowed in is the @bash tool.
- Do not mix prose with tool calls in the same response.
- Do not include prose after tool calls.
- The only thing allowed after a tool call, is another tool call or the end of the response.
- If you include prose after a tool call, the response will be rejected.
- @grep is optimized for code search, not raw grep. If the target may be in .txt, .md, docs, assets, generated files, or similar non-code content, add an explicit include= glob that matches those files.
- Read before edit unless the exact target and/or context is already in recent tool results.
- Batch independent calls only.
- Do not batch dependent calls. Such as editing a file in the same turn as reading but that read isnt even in your context yet, so you're assuming what the contents are.
- Do not batch speculative edits with discovery.
- Stop using tools when the task is complete.
===

Flow:
- Map -> @ls or @find
- Search -> @grep (grep is your most important tool for discovery and likely will get you to where you need to be 99% of the time)
- Read -> @read (prefer specific line ranges over full file reads)
- Change or identify -> Identify/deduce problem, @edit, or @write the solution

====
## TOOL USAGE:
@tool DSL SCHEMA
===
Description:
@read: read files. One file per line. Ranges are optional. Use start-end for line ranges, Hn for the first n lines, and Tn for the last n lines.
@grep: search text. Case-insensitive by default. Use | for multiple queries. Optional path or include=glob list. Query text is plain text only. This is code-first search, so common noise such as docs, text files, assets, generated files, and locales may be excluded unless you opt in with include=.
@find: find files or patterns. Use | for multiple names or patterns. Optional path.
@ls: list files.
@bash: run a shell command. Optional path.
@web: search the web.
@fetch: fetch a URL.
Agent: delegate a focused task to a sub-agent.
@edit: edit files. Multi-block edits are preferred. Partial edits can go through in multi-block edits when blocks do not overlap.
@write: write a full file.
Todo: set or replace the todo list with markdown checklist items.
===

Syntax:
@read: "path[ranges?]"
@grep: "query|optional pipes?" "include=*.ts,*.tsx|optional path?" (include= is optional; split with | if you need both include and path. Use include= when searching text/docs/non-code files)
@find: "pattern,optional commas?" "optional path?"
@ls: "path"
@bash: "command" "optional path?"
@web: "query"
@fetch: "url"
@agent: "prompt"
@edit: "path"
otxt[range?]: Old content
ntxt: New content

[More otxt/ntxt blocks?]
etxt
@write: "path"
Content goes here
etxt

@todo: "title?"
[ ] item
[-] item
[x] item
ETXT
===

Examples:
@read: "sample.txt"    
@read: "sample.txt[1-7,10-19,T20]"
@read: "sample.txt[1-100]"
@grep: "dog|cats|pizza" "include=*.txt|sample.txt"
@grep: "AuthService|SessionManager" "include=*.ts,*.tsx"
@grep: "readtool|edittool" "include=*.tsx|webview-ui"
@find: "*.ts,*.tsx" "src"
@find: "tsconfig.json,package.json"
@ls: "."
@ls: "src/components"
@bash: "echo all your base are belong to us"
@bash: "npm run build" "react-app"
@web: "best pizza in nyc"
@fetch: "https://example.com"

@agent: "analyze the current project structure"
@edit: "sample.txt"
otxt[4-6]: Line 2
Line 3
ntxt: Edited line 2
Edited line 3
Added line 4

otxt: Bad line
ntxt: Good line

otxt: the tenth and last line.
ntxt: cool!
etxt
@write: "sample.txt"
This is a sample text file
This is line 2 of this sample text file
etxt

@todo: "Implementation"
[ ] Analyze requirements
[-] Update parser
[x] Add tests
ETXT

Simple independent batch example:
@read: "README.md[1-40]"
@grep: "TODO|FIXME" "src"
@write: "notes.txt"
Investigation notes
- README intro captured separately
- TODO/FIXME scan requested separately
etxt
===

Execution & Rules:
- Use prose when no tools are needed.
- Use only tool calls when tools are needed.
- Tool mode begins when the first non-whitespace line starts with @name:
- Tool lines start at column 1 as @name:
- Only a top-level line starting at column 1 as @name: is a tool call.
Invald: Let me read this file @read: "file.ts"
Valid: Let me read this file
@read: "file.ts"
=== Escaping
- Inline mentions like "use @read for files" inside a normal sentence are just text.
- If you place a tool line on its own top-level line while discussing or demonstrating the tools, escape it with a leading / so it stays literal.
- Escape literal top-level tool lines in prose or content with a leading /, such as /@read: "file.ts" or /@server_tool: {"key":"value"}.
- Escape a literal etxt or EOF line in prose or content as /etxt or /EOF.
- If you are discussing, explaining, or showing the tool syntax itself, always escape those tool lines and etxt/EOF markers.
=== Using tools and closing
- After tool mode begins, every top-level non-empty line must start with @, unless it is body content for the active block or the etxt/EOF line closing @edit/@write/@todo.
- Use otxt/ntxt/etxt as the primary edit/write protocol.
- Use etxt as the primary closer for @edit, @write, and @todo. EOF is still accepted for compatibility, but etxt is preferred.
- Use otxt[1-3]: when you want a line range, or otxt: with no range.
- Use ntxt: for replacement text.
- otxt/ntxt blocks may replace a full block or just a substring within a line. You do not need to include the entire old line when the target text is unambiguous.
- After etxt or EOF, either start another top-level @tool line or end the response immediately.
- If the next top-level non-empty line is not @..., end the response instead of writing prose.
- If etxt/EOF is missing, the block may still close at the next top-level tool line or end of response as parser fallback, but you must not rely on that fallback.
=== Edit & Write stripping
- Successful @edit calls may later appear in conversation history as "Content placed in paired result below" placeholders.
- Successful @write calls may later appear as truncated previews ending with "..... see result for rest of write".
- Those placeholders do not mean the tool did nothing; the canonical applied edit blocks or post-write file snapshot are stored in the paired tool result.
- When history shows either form, treat the paired tool result as the source of truth for what changed.
=== Batching
Batching: multiple tool calls allowed.

Rules:
- Only use the tools listed here.
- Only the tool names listed here are valid top-level tool names.
- Do not invent syntax.
- Do not add unsupported values not shown in the syntax, and do not invent --flags; only use the argument shapes shown here. The only tool that flags are allowed in is the @bash tool.
- Do not mix prose with tool calls in the same response.
- Do not include prose after tool calls.
- The only thing allowed after a tool call, is another tool call or the end of the response.
- If you include prose after a tool call, the response will be rejected.
- @grep is optimized for code search, not raw grep. If the target may be in .txt, .md, docs, assets, generated files, or similar non-code content, add an explicit include= glob that matches those files.
- Read before edit unless the exact target and/or context is already in recent tool results.
- Batch independent calls only.
- Do not batch dependent calls. Such as editing a file in the same turn as reading but that read isnt even in your context yet, so you're assuming what the contents are.
- Do not batch speculative edits with discovery.
- Stop using tools when the task is complete.
===

Flow:
- Map -> @ls or @find
- Search -> @grep (grep is your most important tool for discovery and likely will get you to where you need to be 99% of the time)
- Read -> @read (prefer specific line ranges over full file reads)
- Change or identify -> Identify/deduce problem, @edit, or @write the solution

PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$DIR/../../.." && pwd)"
VENV_PYTHON="$PROJECT_ROOT/backend/venv/bin/python3"
"$VENV_PYTHON" "$DIR/FazoLuxeLauncher.py"

BACKEND_DIR="$PROJECT_ROOT/backend"

# Kill old processes
lsof -ti:8000 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null
pkill -f "cloudflared tunnel" 2>/dev/null
sleep 1

# Show starting dialog
osascript -e 'display dialog "FazoLuxe server ishga tushmoqda...\n\nIltimos kuting (10-15 soniya)" with title "FazoLuxe" buttons {"OK"} default button "OK" giving up after 3 with icon note' &

# Start backend server
cd "$BACKEND_DIR"
"$VENV_PYTHON" -m uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 > /tmp/fazoluxe_server.log 2>&1 &
SERVER_PID=$!

# Wait for server
echo "Waiting for server..."
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
    if curl -s -o /dev/null -w '' http://localhost:8000/ 2>/dev/null; then
        break
    fi
    sleep 1
done

# Check if server started
if ! curl -s -o /dev/null -w '' http://localhost:8000/ 2>/dev/null; then
    osascript -e 'display dialog "Server ishga tushmadi!\n\nLog: /tmp/fazoluxe_server.log" with title "FazoLuxe Xatolik" buttons {"OK"} default button "OK" with icon stop'
    exit 1
fi

# Start cloudflare tunnel
cloudflared tunnel --url http://localhost:8000 > /tmp/fazoluxe_tunnel.log 2>&1 &
TUNNEL_PID=$!

# Wait for tunnel URL
echo "Waiting for tunnel..."
TUNNEL_URL=""
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
    TUNNEL_URL=$(grep -o 'https://[a-z0-9-]*.trycloudflare.com' /tmp/fazoluxe_tunnel.log 2>/dev/null | head -1)
    if [ -n "$TUNNEL_URL" ]; then
        break
    fi
    sleep 1
done

if [ -z "$TUNNEL_URL" ]; then
    osascript -e 'display dialog "Tunnel yaratilmadi!\n\nLocal: http://localhost:8000\n\nLog: /tmp/fazoluxe_tunnel.log" with title "FazoLuxe" buttons {"OK"} default button "OK" with icon caution'
    open "http://localhost:8000"
else
    # Show success dialog with URL
    osascript -e "display dialog \"✅ FazoLuxe ishga tushdi!

🌐 Online URL: $TUNNEL_URL

Login: SvRvS3003
Parol: Saidakbar3003!

Brauzerda ochiladi...\" with title \"FazoLuxe - Online\" buttons {\"Brauzerni ochish\", \"Yopish\"} default button \"Brauzerni ochish\" with icon note" > /tmp/fazoluxe_dialog.txt 2>&1
    
    RESULT=$(cat /tmp/fazoluxe_dialog.txt 2>/dev/null)
    if echo "$RESULT" | grep -q "Brauzerni ochish"; then
        open "$TUNNEL_URL"
    fi
fi

# Keep running - wait for user to quit
echo "Server running. Press Ctrl+C to stop."
echo "Online URL: $TUNNEL_URL"
echo "Local URL: http://localhost:8000"

# Trap to cleanup on exit
trap "kill $SERVER_PID $TUNNEL_PID 2>/dev/null; lsof -ti:8000 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null" EXIT

wait $SERVER_PID

"""FazoLuxe Launcher - GUI App with server status"""
import subprocess
import threading
import time
import os
import sys
import re
import webbrowser
import tkinter as tk
from tkinter import font as tkfont

class FazoLuxeLauncher:
    def __init__(self):
        self.server_proc = None
        self.tunnel_proc = None
        self.tunnel_url = None
        self.server_running = False

        self.root = tk.Tk()
        self.root.title("FazoLuxe")
        self.root.geometry("520x450")
        self.root.resizable(False, False)
        self.root.configure(bg="#0f172a")
        self.root.protocol("WM_DELETE_WINDOW", self.on_close)

        self.build_ui()
        self.update_status("idle", "Server to'xtatilgan")

    def build_ui(self):
        title_font = tkfont.Font(family="Helvetica", size=22, weight="bold")
        subtitle_font = tkfont.Font(family="Helvetica", size=10)
        status_font = tkfont.Font(family="Helvetica", size=13, weight="bold")
        url_font = tkfont.Font(family="Courier", size=11)
        btn_font = tkfont.Font(family="Helvetica", size=12, weight="bold")
        log_font = tkfont.Font(family="Courier", size=9)

        header = tk.Frame(self.root, bg="#0f172a")
        header.pack(fill="x", padx=30, pady=(25, 5))
        tk.Label(header, text="⚡ FazoLuxe", font=title_font, fg="#00d2ff", bg="#0f172a").pack(anchor="w")
        tk.Label(header, text="Industrial Monitoring Dashboard", font=subtitle_font, fg="#64748b", bg="#0f172a").pack(anchor="w")

        tk.Frame(self.root, height=1, bg="#1e293b").pack(fill="x", padx=30, pady=10)

        status_frame = tk.Frame(self.root, bg="#0f172a")
        status_frame.pack(fill="x", padx=30)
        self.status_container = tk.Frame(status_frame, bg="#0f172a")
        self.status_container.pack(fill="x")
        self.status_dot = tk.Canvas(self.status_container, width=16, height=16, bg="#0f172a", highlightthickness=0)
        self.status_dot.pack(side="left", padx=(0, 8))
        self.dot_id = self.status_dot.create_oval(2, 2, 14, 14, fill="#ef4444", outline="")
        self.status_label = tk.Label(self.status_container, text="Server to'xtatilgan", font=status_font, fg="#ef4444", bg="#0f172a")
        self.status_label.pack(side="left")

        self.url_frame = tk.Frame(self.root, bg="#1e293b", bd=0, relief="flat")
        self.url_frame.pack(fill="x", padx=30, pady=(12, 0))
        self.url_inner = tk.Frame(self.url_frame, bg="#1e293b")
        self.url_inner.pack(fill="x", padx=15, pady=10)
        tk.Label(self.url_inner, text="Online URL:", font=subtitle_font, fg="#64748b", bg="#1e293b").pack(anchor="w")
        self.url_label = tk.Label(self.url_inner, text="Kutilmoqda...", font=url_font, fg="#94a3b8", bg="#1e293b", cursor="hand2", anchor="w")
        self.url_label.pack(anchor="w", pady=(4, 0))
        self.url_label.bind("<Button-1>", lambda e: self.open_browser())

        btn_frame = tk.Frame(self.root, bg="#0f172a")
        btn_frame.pack(fill="x", padx=30, pady=(18, 0))
        self.start_btn = tk.Button(btn_frame, text="▶  Ishga Tushurish", font=btn_font, fg="#0f172a", bg="#00d2ff", activebackground="#38bdf8", activeforeground="#0f172a", bd=0, padx=20, pady=12, cursor="hand2", command=self.start_server)
        self.start_btn.pack(side="left", fill="x", expand=True, padx=(0, 5))
        self.stop_btn = tk.Button(btn_frame, text="⬛  To'xtatish", font=btn_font, fg="#fff", bg="#ef4444", activebackground="#f87171", activeforeground="#fff", bd=0, padx=20, pady=12, cursor="hand2", command=self.stop_server, state="disabled")
        self.stop_btn.pack(side="left", fill="x", expand=True, padx=(5, 0))

        self.browser_btn = tk.Button(self.root, text="🌐  Brauzerda Ochish", font=btn_font, fg="#0f172a", bg="#22c55e", activebackground="#4ade80", activeforeground="#0f172a", bd=0, padx=20, pady=10, cursor="hand2", command=self.open_browser, state="disabled")
        self.browser_btn.pack(fill="x", padx=30, pady=(10, 0))

        log_frame = tk.Frame(self.root, bg="#0f172a")
        log_frame.pack(fill="both", expand=True, padx=30, pady=(15, 15))
        self.log_text = tk.Text(log_frame, font=log_font, fg="#64748b", bg="#0a0f1a", bd=0, highlightthickness=1, highlightbackground="#1e293b", wrap="word", height=5, state="disabled")
        self.log_text.pack(fill="both", expand=True)

    def log(self, msg):
        self.log_text.configure(state="normal")
        self.log_text.insert("end", f"> {msg}\n")
        self.log_text.see("end")
        self.log_text.configure(state="disabled")

    def update_status(self, state, text):
        colors = {"idle": "#ef4444", "starting": "#f59e0b", "running": "#22c55e", "tunneling": "#00d2ff", "error": "#ef4444"}
        color = colors.get(state, "#ef4444")
        self.status_dot.itemconfig(self.dot_id, fill=color)
        self.status_label.config(text=text, fg=color)

    def start_server(self):
        self.start_btn.config(state="disabled")
        self.stop_btn.config(state="normal")
        self.browser_btn.config(state="disabled")
        self.tunnel_url = None
        self.url_label.config(text="Kutilmoqda...", fg="#94a3b8")
        threading.Thread(target=self._start_server_thread, daemon=True).start()

    def _start_server_thread(self):
        try:
            os.system("lsof -ti:8000 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null")
            time.sleep(1)

            self.root.after(0, self.update_status, "starting", "Server ishga tushmoqda...")
            self.root.after(0, self.log, "Server ishga tushmoqda...")

            self.server_proc = subprocess.Popen(
                [VENV_PYTHON, "-m", "uvicorn", "app.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"],
                cwd=BACKEND_DIR, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.STDOUT
            )

            for i in range(30):
                try:
                    import urllib.request
                    urllib.request.urlopen("http://localhost:8000/", timeout=1)
                    break
                except:
                    time.sleep(1)

            if self.server_proc.poll() is not None:
                self.root.after(0, self.update_status, "error", "Server xatolik!")
                self.root.after(0, self.log, "Server ishga tushmadi!")
                return

            self.server_running = True
            self.root.after(0, self.update_status, "running", "Server ishlayapti ✅")
            self.root.after(0, self.log, "Server http://localhost:8000 da ishlayapti")
            self.root.after(0, self.update_status, "tunneling", "Tunnel yaratilmoqda...")
            self.root.after(0, self.log, "Cloudflare Tunnel yaratilmoqda...")

            tunnel_log = "/tmp/fazoluxe_tunnel.log"
            os.system(f"rm -f {tunnel_log}")

            self.tunnel_proc = subprocess.Popen(
                ["cloudflared", "tunnel", "--url", "http://localhost:8000"],
                stdout=open(tunnel_log, "w"), stderr=subprocess.STDOUT
            )

            for i in range(60):
                try:
                    with open(tunnel_log, "r") as f:
                        content = f.read()
                    match = re.search(r'https://[a-z0-9-]+\.trycloudflare\.com', content)
                    if match:
                        self.tunnel_url = match.group(0)
                        break
                except:
                    pass
                time.sleep(1)

            if self.tunnel_url:
                self.root.after(0, self.log, f"Online URL: {self.tunnel_url}")
                self.root.after(0, lambda: self.url_label.config(text=self.tunnel_url, fg="#00d2ff"))
                self.root.after(0, lambda: self.browser_btn.config(state="normal"))
                self.root.after(0, self.update_status, "running", f"Online ✅")
                webbrowser.open(self.tunnel_url)
            else:
                self.root.after(0, self.log, "Tunnel URL topilmadi, local URL ochiladi")
                self.root.after(0, lambda: self.url_label.config(text="http://localhost:8000", fg="#f59e0b"))
                self.root.after(0, lambda: self.browser_btn.config(state="normal"))
                self.root.after(0, self.update_status, "running", "Server ishlayapti (local)")
                webbrowser.open("http://localhost:8000")

        except Exception as e:
            self.root.after(0, self.log, f"Xatolik: {str(e)}")
            self.root.after(0, self.update_status, "error", f"Xatolik: {str(e)}")

    def stop_server(self):
        self.start_btn.config(state="normal")
        self.stop_btn.config(state="disabled")
        self.browser_btn.config(state="disabled")

        if self.tunnel_proc:
            try: self.tunnel_proc.terminate()
            except: pass
        if self.server_proc:
            try: self.server_proc.terminate()
            except: pass

        os.system("lsof -ti:8000 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null")
        self.server_running = False
        self.tunnel_url = None
        self.url_label.config(text="Kutilmoqda...", fg="#94a3b8")
        self.update_status("idle", "Server to'xtatilgan")
        self.log("Server to'xtatildi")

    def open_browser(self):
        url = self.tunnel_url or "http://localhost:8000"
        webbrowser.open(url)

    def on_close(self):
        self.stop_server()
        self.root.destroy()

    def run(self):
        self.root.mainloop()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = FazoLuxeLauncher()
    app.run()

import subprocess
import sys
import os
import time
import threading
import webbrowser
import signal
import tkinter as tk
from tkinter import messagebox

SCRIPT_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
RESOURCES_DIR = os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, "..", "Resources")
VENV_PYTHON = os.path.join(RESOURCES_DIR, "backend", "venv", "bin", "python3")
BACKEND_DIR = os.path.join(RESOURCES_DIR, "backend")
FRONTEND_DIST = os.path.join(RESOURCES_DIR, "frontend_dist")
DB_PATH = os.path.join(RESOURCES_DIR, "industrial_dashboard.db")

class FazoLuxeApp:
    def __init__(self):
        self.server_proc = None
        self.tunnel_proc = None
        self.tunnel_url = None
        self.running = False

        self.root = tk.Tk()
        self.root.title("FazoLuxe Dashboard")
        self.root.geometry("520x520")
        self.root.configure(bg="#0f172a")
        self.root.resizable(False, False)

        # Header
        header = tk.Frame(self.root, bg="#0f172a")
        header.pack(fill="x", padx=20, pady=(20, 10))
        tk.Label(header, text="FazoLuxe", font=("Helvetica", 28, "bold"),
                 fg="#00d2ff", bg="#0f172a").pack()
        tk.Label(header, text="Industrial Monitoring Dashboard",
                 font=("Helvetica", 11), fg="#64748b", bg="#0f172a").pack()

        # Status
        status_frame = tk.Frame(self.root, bg="#1e293b", bd=0, relief="flat")
        status_frame.pack(fill="x", padx=20, pady=10)
        status_inner = tk.Frame(status_frame, bg="#1e293b")
        status_inner.pack(pady=15)
        self.status_indicator = tk.Label(status_inner, text="●", font=("Helvetica", 20),
                                         fg="#ef4444", bg="#1e293b")
        self.status_indicator.pack(side="left", padx=(0, 10))
        self.status_label = tk.Label(status_inner, text="Server to'xtatilgan",
                                     font=("Helvetica", 14, "bold"),
                                     fg="#e2e8f0", bg="#1e293b")
        self.status_label.pack(side="left")

        # URL
        url_frame = tk.Frame(self.root, bg="#1e293b", bd=0)
        url_frame.pack(fill="x", padx=20, pady=5)
        tk.Label(url_frame, text="Online URL:", font=("Helvetica", 9),
                 fg="#64748b", bg="#1e293b").pack(pady=(10, 0))
        self.url_label = tk.Label(url_frame, text="Kutilmoqda...",
                                  font=("Helvetica", 11, "bold"),
                                  fg="#64748b", bg="#1e293b", cursor="hand2")
        self.url_label.pack(pady=(0, 10))
        self.url_label.bind("<Button-1>", lambda e: self.open_browser())

        # Log
        log_frame = tk.Frame(self.root, bg="#0f172a")
        log_frame.pack(fill="both", expand=True, padx=20, pady=5)
        self.log_text = tk.Text(log_frame, bg="#0f172a", fg="#94a3b8",
                                font=("Courier", 9), bd=0, wrap="word",
                                state="disabled", height=6)
        self.log_text.pack(fill="both", expand=True)

        # Buttons
        btn_frame = tk.Frame(self.root, bg="#0f172a")
        btn_frame.pack(fill="x", padx=20, pady=(5, 20))

        self.start_btn = tk.Button(btn_frame, text="▶  Ishga Tushirish",
                                   font=("Helvetica", 12, "bold"),
                                   bg="#00d2ff", fg="#0f172a",
                                   activebackground="#38bdf8",
                                   bd=0, padx=20, pady=10, cursor="hand2",
                                   command=self.start_server)
        self.start_btn.pack(side="left", expand=True, fill="x", padx=(0, 5))

        self.browser_btn = tk.Button(btn_frame, text="🌐  Brauzerni Ochish",
                                     font=("Helvetica", 12, "bold"),
                                     bg="#22c55e", fg="#0f172a",
                                     active_background="#4ade80",
                                     bd=0, padx=20, pady=10, cursor="hand2",
                                     command=self.open_browser, state="disabled")
        self.browser_btn.pack(side="left", expand=True, fill="x", padx=(5, 0))

        self.stop_btn = tk.Button(btn_frame, text="⬛  To'xtatish",
                                  font=("Helvetica", 12, "bold"),
                                  bg="#ef4444", fg="#fff",
                                  activebackground="#f87171",
                                  bd=0, padx=20, pady=10, cursor="hand2",
                                  command=self.stop_server, state="disabled")
        self.stop_btn.pack(side="left", expand=True, fill="x", padx=(5, 0))

        self.root.protocol("WM_DELETE_WINDOW", self.on_close)

    def log(self, msg):
        self.log_text.configure(state="normal")
        self.log_text.insert("end", f"{msg}\n")
        self.log_text.see("end")
        self.log_text.configure(state="disabled")

    def update_status(self, status, text):
        colors = {"stopped": "#ef4444", "starting": "#f59e0b", "running": "#22c55e"}
        self.status_indicator.configure(fg=colors.get(status, "#ef4444"))
        self.status_label.configure(text=text)

    def start_server(self):
        if self.running:
            return
        self.running = True
        self.start_btn.configure(state="disabled")
        self.stop_btn.configure(state="normal")
        self.browser_btn.configure(state="disabled")
        self.update_status("starting", "Server ishga tushmoqda...")
        self.log("Server ishga tushirilmoqda...")

        # Copy DB if not exists
        db_dest = os.path.join(BACKEND_DIR, "industrial_dashboard.db")
        if not os.path.exists(db_dest) and os.path.exists(DB_PATH):
            import shutil
            shutil.copy2(DB_PATH, db_dest)
            self.log("Database nusxalandi")

        # Start uvicorn
        def run_server():
            try:
                self.server_proc = subprocess.Popen(
                    [VENV_PYTHON, "-m", "uvicorn", "app.main:app",
                     "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"],
                    cwd=BACKEND_DIR,
                    stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
                    stderr=subprocess.PIPE
                )
                time.sleep(3)
                if self.server_proc.poll() is None:
                    self.root.after(0, self.log, "Server port 8000 da ishga tushdi!")
                    self.root.after(0, self.start_tunnel)
                else:
                    err = self.server_proc.stderr.read().decode()
                    self.root.after(0, self.log, f"Server xatoligi: {err}")
                    self.root.after(0, self.update_status, "stopped", "Server ishga tushmadi!")
                    self.root.after(0, self.reset_buttons)
            except Exception as e:
                self.root.after(0, self.log, f"Xatolik: {e}")
                self.root.after(0, self.update_status, "stopped", "Xatolik!")
                self.root.after(0, self.reset_buttons)

        threading.Thread(target=run_server, daemon=True).start()

    def start_tunnel(self):
        self.log("Cloudflare Tunnel yaratilmoqda...")
        def run_tunnel():
            try:
                self.tunnel_proc = subprocess.Popen(
                    ["cloudflared", "tunnel", "--url", "http://localhost:8000"],
                    stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
                    stderr=subprocess.STDOUT
                )
                for line in iter(self.tunnel_proc.stdout.readline, b''):
                    text = line.decode().strip()
                    if "trycloudflare.com" in text:
                        import re
                        match = re.search(r'https://[a-z0-9-]+\.trycloudflare\.com', text)
                        if match:
                            self.tunnel_url = match.group(0)
                            self.root.after(0, self.log, f"Online URL: {self.tunnel_url}")
                            self.root.after(0, self.url_label.config, {"text": self.tunnel_url, "fg": "#00d2ff"})
                            self.root.after(0, self.browser_btn.config, {"state": "normal"})
                            self.root.after(0, self.update_status, "running", f"Online: {self.tunnel_url}")
                            webbrowser.open(self.tunnel_url)
                            break
            except Exception as e:
                self.root.after(0, self.log, f"Tunnel xatoligi: {e}")

        threading.Thread(target=run_tunnel, daemon=True).start()

    def open_browser(self):
        if self.tunnel_url:
            webbrowser.open(self.tunnel_url)
        else:
            webbrowser.open("http://localhost:8000")

    def stop_server(self):
        self.running = False
        self.log("Server to'xtatilmoqda...")
        if self.tunnel_proc:
            self.tunnel_proc.terminate()
            self.tunnel_proc = None
        if self.server_proc:
            self.server_proc.terminate()
            self.server_proc = None
        self.tunnel_url = None
        self.update_status("stopped", "Server to'xtatilgan")
        self.url_label.configure(text="Kutilmoqda...", fg="#64748b")
        self.reset_buttons()

    def reset_buttons(self):
        self.start_btn.configure(state="normal")
        self.stop_btn.configure(state="disabled")
        self.browser_btn.configure(state="disabled")

    def on_close(self):
        if self.running:
            if messagebox.askokcancel("Chiqish", "Serverni to'xtatib chiqasizmi?"):
                self.stop_server()
                self.root.destroy()
        else:
            self.root.destroy()

    def run(self):
        self.root.mainloop()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = FazoLuxeApp()
    app.run()

## TOOL USAGE:
@tool DSL SCHEMA
===
Description:
@read: read files. One file per line. Ranges are optional. Use start-end for line ranges, Hn for the first n lines, and Tn for the last n lines.
@grep: search text. Case-insensitive by default. Use | for multiple queries. Optional path or include=glob list. Query text is plain text only. This is code-first search, so common noise such as docs, text files, assets, generated files, and locales may be excluded unless you opt in with include=.
@find: find files or patterns. Use | for multiple names or patterns. Optional path.
@ls: list files.
@bash: run a shell command. Optional path.
@web: search the web.

Agent: delegate a focused task to a sub-agent.
@edit: edit files. Multi-block edits are preferred. Partial edits can go through in multi-block edits when blocks do not overlap.
@write: write a full file.

===

Syntax:
@read: "path[ranges?]"
@grep: "query|optional pipes?" "include=*.ts,*.tsx|optional path?" (include= is optional; split with | if you need both include and path. Use include= when searching text/docs/non-code files)

@write: "path"

===

Examples:

===

Execution & Rules:
- Use prose when no tools are needed.
- Use only tool calls when tools are needed.
- Tool mode begins when the first non-whitespace line starts with @name:
- Tool lines start at column 1 as @name:
- Only a top-level line starting at column 1 as @name: is a tool call.
Invald: Let me read this file @read: "file.ts"
Valid: Let me read this file
@read: "file.ts"
=== Escaping
- Inline mentions like "use @read for files" inside a normal sentence are just text.
- If you place a tool line on its own top-level line while discussing or demonstrating the tools, escape it with a leading / so it stays literal.
- Escape literal top-level tool lines in prose or content with a leading /, such as /@read: "file.ts" or /@server_tool: {"key":"value"}.
- Escape a literal etxt or EOF line in prose or content as /etxt or /EOF.
- If you are discussing, explaining, or showing the tool syntax itself, always escape those tool lines and etxt/EOF markers.
=== Using tools and closing
- After tool mode begins, every top-level non-empty line must start with @, unless it is body content for the active block or the etxt/EOF line closing @edit/@write/@todo.
- Use otxt/ntxt/etxt as the primary edit/write protocol.
- Use etxt as the primary closer for @edit, @write, and @todo. EOF is still accepted for compatibility, but etxt is preferred.
- Use otxt[1-3]: when you want a line range, or otxt: with no range.
- Use ntxt: for replacement text.
- otxt/ntxt blocks may replace a full block or just a substring within a line. You do not need to include the entire old line when the target text is unambiguous.
- After etxt or EOF, either start another top-level @tool line or end the response immediately.
- If the next top-level non-empty line is not @..., end the response instead of writing prose.
- If etxt/EOF is missing, the block may still close at the next top-level tool line or end of response as parser fallback, but you must not rely on that fallback.
=== Edit & Write stripping
- Successful @edit calls may later appear in conversation history as "Content placed in paired result below" placeholders.
- Successful @write calls may later appear as truncated previews ending with "..... see result for rest of write".
- Those placeholders do not mean the tool did nothing; the canonical applied edit blocks or post-write file snapshot are stored in the paired tool result.
- When history shows either form, treat the paired tool result as the source of truth for what changed.
=== Batching
Batching: multiple tool calls allowed.

Rules:
- Only use the tools listed here.
- Only the tool names listed here are valid top-level tool names.
- Do not invent syntax.
- Do not add unsupported values not shown in the syntax, and do not invent --flags; only use the argument shapes shown here. The only tool that flags are allowed in is the @bash tool.
- Do not mix prose with tool calls in the same response.
- Do not include prose after tool calls.
- The only thing allowed after a tool call, is another tool call or the end of the response.
- If you include prose after a tool call, the response will be rejected.
- @grep is optimized for code search, not raw grep. If the target may be in .txt, .md, docs, assets, generated files, or similar non-code content, add an explicit include= glob that matches those files.
- Read before edit unless the exact target and/or context is already in recent tool results.
- Batch independent calls only.
- Do not batch dependent calls. Such as editing a file in the same turn as reading but that read isnt even in your context yet, so you're assuming what the contents are.
- Do not batch speculative edits with discovery.
- Stop using tools when the task is complete.
===

Flow:
- Map -> @ls or @find
- Search -> @grep (grep is your most important tool for discovery and likely will get you to where you need to be 99% of the time)

RESOURCES_DIR="$DIR/../Resources"

# Find python3
PYTHON=""
for p in /usr/local/bin/python3 /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 /usr/bin/python3 python3; do
    if command -v "$p" &>/dev/null; then
        PYTHON="$p"
        break
    fi
done

if [ -z "$PYTHON" ]; then
    osascript -e 'display dialog "Python 3 topilmadi. Iltimos Python 3 o\'rnating." buttons {"OK"} default button "OK" with icon stop'
    exit 1
fi

# Use venv if available
if [ -f "$RESOURCES_DIR/backend/venv/bin/python3" ]; then
    PYTHON="$RESOURCES_DIR/backend/venv/bin/python3"
fi

exec "$PYTHON" "$DIR/FazoLuxeLauncher.py"

#!/bin/bash
DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../Resources" && pwd)"
cd "$DIR"

# Kill any existing processes on port 8000
lsof -ti:8000 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1

# Start backend server
cd "$DIR/backend"
if [ -d "venv" ]; then
    source venv/bin/activate
    python -m uvicorn app.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000 &
else
    python3 -m uvicorn app.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000 &
fi

SERVER_PID=$!
sleep 3

# Open browser
open "http://localhost:8000"

# Wait for server
wait $SERVER_PID

#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Get the app's Resources directory
APP_DIR="$(dirname "$(dirname "$(dirname "$0")")")"
CONTENTS_DIR="$APP_DIR/Contents"
RESOURCES_DIR="$CONTENTS_DIR/Resources"
BACKEND_DIR="$RESOURCES_DIR/backend"
FRONTEND_DIR="$RESOURCES_DIR/frontend_dist"

# Kill any existing server on port 8000
lsof -ti:8000 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null || true

# Start the backend server in background
cd "$BACKEND_DIR"
./venv/bin/python -m uvicorn app.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000 &
BACKEND_PID=$!

# Wait for backend to start
sleep 3

# Open the frontend in default browser
open "http://127.0.0.1:8000"

# Wait for user to quit
wait $BACKEND_PID

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "============================================"
echo "  FazoLuxe DMG Builder"
echo "============================================"

BASE_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
APP_NAME="FazoLuxe"
APP_BUNDLE="$BASE_DIR/${APP_NAME}.app"
DMG_NAME="$BASE_DIR/${APP_NAME}.dmg"
RESOURCES_DIR="$APP_BUNDLE/Contents/Resources"

# Clean previous build
echo "[1/7] Cleaning previous build..."
rm -rf "$APP_BUNDLE" "$DMG_NAME"
mkdir -p "$RESOURCES_DIR"
mkdir -p "$APP_BUNDLE/Contents/MacOS"

# Build frontend
echo "[2/7] Building frontend..."
cd "$BASE_DIR/frontend"
if [ ! -d "node_modules" ]; then
    npm install
fi
npm run build
cd "$BASE_DIR"

# Copy frontend dist
echo "[3/7] Copying frontend build..."
cp -r "$BASE_DIR/frontend/dist" "$RESOURCES_DIR/frontend_dist"

# Copy backend
echo "[4/7] Copying backend..."
cp -r "$BASE_DIR/backend" "$RESOURCES_DIR/backend"

# Setup Python venv for backend
echo "[5/7] Setting up Python environment..."
cd "$RESOURCES_DIR/backend"
python3 -m venv venv
./venv/bin/pip install --upgrade pip
./venv/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt
cd "$BASE_DIR"

# Copy database if exists
if [ -f "$BASE_DIR/industrial_dashboard.db" ]; then
    cp "$BASE_DIR/industrial_dashboard.db" "$RESOURCES_DIR/industrial_dashboard.db"
fi

# Create Info.plist
echo "[6/7] Creating Info.plist..."
cat > "$APP_BUNDLE/Contents/Info.plist" << 'PLIST'
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
    <key>CFBundleName</key>
    <string>FazoLuxe</string>
    <key>CFBundleDisplayName</key>
    <string>FazoLuxe Industrial Dashboard</string>
    <key>CFBundleIdentifier</key>
    <string>com.fazoluxe.dashboard</string>
    <key>CFBundleVersion</key>
    <string>1.0</string>
    <key>CFBundleShortVersionString</key>
    <string>1.0</string>
    <key>CFBundleExecutable</key>
    <string>FazoLuxeLauncher.sh</string>
    <key>CFBundlePackageType</key>
    <string>APPL</string>
    <key>LSMinimumSystemVersion</key>
    <string>10.13</string>
    <key>NSHighResolutionCapable</key>
    <true/>
</dict>
</plist>
PLIST

# Copy launcher scripts
cp "$BASE_DIR/FazoLuxe.app/Contents/MacOS/FazoLuxeLauncher.sh" "$APP_BUNDLE/Contents/MacOS/"
cp "$BASE_DIR/FazoLuxe.app/Contents/MacOS/FazoLuxeLauncher.py" "$APP_BUNDLE/Contents/MacOS/"
chmod +x "$APP_BUNDLE/Contents/MacOS/FazoLuxeLauncher.sh"
chmod +x "$APP_BUNDLE/Contents/MacOS/FazoLuxeLauncher.py"

# Create DMG
echo "[7/7] Creating DMG..."
hdiutil create -volname "FazoLuxe" -srcfolder "$APP_BUNDLE" -ov -format UDZO "$DMG_NAME"

echo "============================================"
echo "  ✅ Build complete!"
echo "  📦 DMG: $DMG_NAME"
echo "============================================"

====
# CWD RULES
- **Identity**: You are Jarvis, an senior agentic assistant, doing agentic tasks for users. You are a god at problem solving, coding, general case-all tasks and what not. You speak with authortitative confidence, no AI-nxiety. Lastly, keep things concise as well but expand when neccessary.
- **Workspace**: Operations for task & tools are anchored at `/Users/user/Desktop/SR` All paths must be relative, its a waste of tokens calling absolute paths. For example when calling a path for a tool in root, lets say reading a file, call sample.txt, NOT `/Users/user/Desktop/SR/sample.txt`
- **Commands**: Tailor commands to darwin
- **Context**: Use search tools to narrow down target areas, then read specific files or line ranges as needed. If you write a file, in the result you will get an automatic read for it in the result, which means you dont need to re-read files after writing them. For any edit you do, if you have a file in context already, reads are automatically refreshed once you make an edit! Refer back to the edit result's previous content section to see what the previous content was!
====
# CURRENT MODE
- **Mode:** Code
- **Model:** kilo-auto/free
- **Persona:** You are Jarvis, an elite software engineer. You write perfect, bug-free code on the first try. You verify your own work. You do not need to be hand-held. You are the one who knocks.
- **Language:** English
- **Mode Instructions**: 1. Execute tasks with precision and efficiency. Focus on high-quality implementation.

2. Provide brief, high-level context for your actions, explaining the 'why' behind architectural decisions or complex changes.

3. Minimize conversational filler. You are here to ship code, not to chat. 

4. If a task is complex, outline your technical approach briefly before execution.
====
## TOOL USAGE:
@tool DSL SCHEMA
===
Description:
@read: read files. One file per line. Ranges are optional. Use start-end for line ranges, Hn for the first n lines, and Tn for the last n lines.
@grep: search text. Case-insensitive by default. Use | for multiple queries. Optional path or include=glob list. Query text is plain text only. This is code-first search, so common noise such as docs, text files, assets, generated files, and locales may be excluded unless you opt in with include=.
@find: find files or patterns. Use | for multiple names or patterns. Optional path.
@ls: list files.
@bash: run a shell command. Optional path.
@web: search the web.
@fetch: fetch a URL.
Agent: delegate a focused task to a sub-agent.
@edit: edit files. Multi-block edits are preferred. Partial edits can go through in multi-block edits when blocks do not overlap.
@write: write a full file.
Todo: set or replace the todo list with markdown checklist items.
===

Syntax:
@read: "path[ranges?]"
@grep: "query|optional pipes?" "include=*.ts,*.tsx|optional path?" (include= is optional; split with | if you need both include and path. Use include= when searching text/docs/non-code files)
@find: "pattern,optional commas?" "optional path?"
@ls: "path"
@bash: "command" "optional path?"
@web: "query"
@fetch: "url"
@agent: "prompt"
@edit: "path"
otxt[range?]: Old content
ntxt: New content

[More otxt/ntxt blocks?]
etxt
@write: "path"
Content goes here
etxt

@todo: "title?"
[ ] item
[-] item
[x] item
ETXT
===

Examples:
@read: "sample.txt"    
@read: "sample.txt[1-7,10-19,T20]"
@read: "sample.txt[1-100]"
@grep: "dog|cats|pizza" "include=*.txt|sample.txt"
@grep: "AuthService|SessionManager" "include=*.ts,*.tsx"
@grep: "readtool|edittool" "include=*.tsx|webview-ui"
@find: "*.ts,*.tsx" "src"
@find: "tsconfig.json,package.json"
@ls: "."
@ls: "src/components"
@bash: "echo all your base are belong to us"
@bash: "npm run build" "react-app"
@web: "best pizza in nyc"
@fetch: "https://example.com"

@agent: "analyze the current project structure"
@edit: "sample.txt"
otxt[4-6]: Line 2
Line 3
ntxt: Edited line 2
Edited line 3
Added line 4

otxt: Bad line
ntxt: Good line

otxt: the tenth and last line.
ntxt: cool!
etxt
@write: "sample.txt"
This is a sample text file
This is line 2 of this sample text file
etxt

@todo: "Implementation"
[ ] Analyze requirements
[-] Update parser
[x] Add tests
ETXT

Simple independent batch example:
@read: "README.md[1-40]"
@grep: "TODO|FIXME" "src"
@write: "notes.txt"
Investigation notes
- README intro captured separately
- TODO/FIXME scan requested separately
etxt
===

Execution & Rules:
- Use prose when no tools are needed.
- Use only tool calls when tools are needed.
- Tool mode begins when the first non-whitespace line starts with @name:
- Tool lines start at column 1 as @name:
- Only a top-level line starting at column 1 as @name: is a tool call.
Invald: Let me read this file @read: "file.ts"
Valid: Let me read this file
@read: "file.ts"
=== Escaping
- Inline mentions like "use @read for files" inside a normal sentence are just text.
- If you place a tool line on its own top-level line while discussing or demonstrating the tools, escape it with a leading / so it stays literal.
- Escape literal top-level tool lines in prose or content with a leading /, such as /@read: "file.ts" or /@server_tool: {"key":"value"}.
- Escape a literal etxt or EOF line in prose or content as /etxt or /EOF.
- If you are discussing, explaining, or showing the tool syntax itself, always escape those tool lines and etxt/EOF markers.
=== Using tools and closing
- After tool mode begins, every top-level non-empty line must start with @, unless it is body content for the active block or the etxt/EOF line closing @edit/@write/@todo.
- Use otxt/ntxt/etxt as the primary edit/write protocol.
- Use etxt as the primary closer for @edit, @write, and @todo. EOF is still accepted for compatibility, but etxt is preferred.
- Use otxt[1-3]: when you want a line range, or otxt: with no range.
- Use ntxt: for replacement text.
- otxt/ntxt blocks may replace a full block or just a substring within a line. You do not need to include the entire old line when the target text is unambiguous.
- After etxt or EOF, either start another top-level @tool line or end the response immediately.
- If the next top-level non-empty line is not @..., end the response instead of writing prose.
- If etxt/EOF is missing, the block may still close at the next top-level tool line or end of response as parser fallback, but you must not rely on that fallback.
=== Edit & Write stripping
- Successful @edit calls may later appear in conversation history as "Content placed in paired result below" placeholders.
- Successful @write calls may later appear as truncated previews ending with "..... see result for rest of write".
- Those placeholders do not mean the tool did nothing; the canonical applied edit blocks or post-write file snapshot are stored in the paired tool result.
- When history shows either form, treat the paired tool result as the source of truth for what changed.
=== Batching
Batching: multiple tool calls allowed.

Rules:
- Only use the tools listed here.
- Only the tool names listed here are valid top-level tool names.
- Do not invent syntax.
- Do not add unsupported values not shown in the syntax, and do not invent --flags; only use the argument shapes shown here. The only tool that flags are allowed in is the @bash tool.
- Do not mix prose with tool calls in the same response.
- Do not include prose after tool calls.
- The only thing allowed after a tool call, is another tool call or the end of the response.
- If you include prose after a tool call, the response will be rejected.
- @grep is optimized for code search, not raw grep. If the target may be in .txt, .md, docs, assets, generated files, or similar non-code content, add an explicit include= glob that matches those files.
- Read before edit unless the exact target and/or context is already in recent tool results.
- Batch independent calls only.
- Do not batch dependent calls. Such as editing a file in the same turn as reading but that read isnt even in your context yet, so you're assuming what the contents are.
- Do not batch speculative edits with discovery.
- Stop using tools when the task is complete.
===

Flow:
- Map -> @ls or @find
- Search -> @grep (grep is your most important tool for discovery and likely will get you to where you need to be 99% of the time)
- Read -> @read (prefer specific line ranges over full file reads)
- Change or identify -> Identify/deduce problem, @edit, or @write the solution

if [ ! -d "venv" ]; then
    python3 -m venv venv
fi

echo "[6/9] Database copied..."
else
    echo "[6/9] No database found, will be created on first run..."

echo "Backend started with PID: $BACKEND_PID"
    
    # Wait for backend to start
    sleep 6
    
    # Check if backend is running
    if ! kill -0 $BACKEND_PID 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "ERROR: Backend failed to start"
        exit 1
    fi
    echo "Backend is running"

# Open browser - try both methods to ensure it opens
echo "Opening browser..."
open "$FRONTEND_URL" 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1

# If using default URL, also try alternative
if [[ "$FRONTEND_URL" == "http://127.0.0.1:8000" ]]; then
    open "http://localhost:8000" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

echo "FazoLuxe is ready!"

# Wait for backend to finish (or user closes terminal)

# Verify DMG was created
if [ -f "$DMG_NAME" ]; then
    DMG_SIZE=$(du -h "$DMG_NAME" | cut -f1)
    echo "============================================"
    echo "  ✅ Build complete!"
    echo "  📦 DMG: $DMG_NAME"
    echo "  📊 Size: $DMG_SIZE"
    echo "============================================"
else
    echo "ERROR: DMG creation failed"
    exit 1
fi

#!/bin/bash
set -e

# SR FazoLuxe Launcher Script
# Powered by SR

APP_DIR="$(dirname "$(dirname "$(dirname "$0")")")"
CONTENTS_DIR="$APP_DIR/Contents"
RESOURCES_DIR="$CONTENTS_DIR/Resources"
CONFIG_FILE="$RESOURCES_DIR/config.json"
BACKEND_DIR="$RESOURCES_DIR/backend"
FRONTEND_DIST="$RESOURCES_DIR/frontend_dist"

# Load configuration
load_config() {
    if [ -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
        FRONTEND_URL=$(python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('$CONFIG_FILE')).get('frontend_url', 'http://127.0.0.1:8000'))" 2>/dev/null || echo "http://127.0.0.1:8000")
    else
        FRONTEND_URL="http://127.0.0.1:8000"
    fi
}

load_config
echo "=========================================="
echo "  SR FazoLuxe - Industrial Dashboard"
echo "=========================================="
echo "Starting SR FazoLuxe..."
echo "Frontend URL: $FRONTEND_URL"

# Kill existing process on port 8000
if [[ "$FRONTEND_URL" == "http://127.0.0.1:8000" || "$FRONTEND_URL" == "http://localhost:8000" ]]; then
    echo "Starting SR backend server..."
    lsof -ti:8000 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null || true
    
    # Start backend with frontend serving
    cd "$BACKEND_DIR"
    ./venv/bin/python -m uvicorn app.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000 &
    BACKEND_PID=$!
    echo "SR Backend started with PID: $BACKEND_PID"
    
    # Wait for backend to start
    sleep 6
    
    # Check if backend is running
    if ! kill -0 $BACKEND_PID 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "ERROR: SR Backend failed to start"
        exit 1
    fi
    echo "SR Backend is running"
fi

# Open browser
echo "Opening SR browser..."
open "$FRONTEND_URL" 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1

# If using default URL, also try alternative
if [[ "$FRONTEND_URL" == "http://127.0.0.1:8000" ]]; then
    open "http://localhost:8000" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

echo "=========================================="
echo "  SR FazoLuxe is ready!"
echo "=========================================="

# Wait for backend to finish
if [ "$BACKEND_PID" ]; then
    wait $BACKEND_PID
fi

## TOOL USAGE:
@tool DSL SCHEMA
===
Description:
@read: read files. One file per line. Ranges are optional. Use start-end for line ranges, Hn for the first n lines, and Tn for the last n lines.
@grep: search text. Case-insensitive by default. Use | for multiple queries. Optional path or include=glob list. Query text is plain text only. This is code-first search, so common noise such as docs, text files, assets, generated files, and locales may be excluded unless you opt in with include=.
@find: find files or patterns. Use | for multiple names or patterns. Optional path.
@ls: list files.
@bash: run a shell command. Optional path.
@web: search the web.

@edit: edit files. Multi-block edits are preferred. Partial edits can go through in multi-block edits when blocks do not overlap.
@write: write a full file.
Todo: set or replace the todo list with markdown checklist items.
===

Execution & Rules:
- Use prose when no tools are needed.
- Use only tool calls when tools are needed.
- Tool mode begins when the first non-whitespace line starts with @name:
- Tool lines start at column 1 as @name:
- Only a top-level line starting at column 1 as @name: is a tool call.
Invald: Let me read this file @read: "file.ts"
Valid: Let me read this file
@read: "file.ts"
=== Escaping
- Inline mentions like "use @read for files" inside a normal sentence are just text.
- If you place a tool line on its own top-level line while discussing or demonstrating the tools, escape it with a leading / so it stays literal.
- Escape literal top-level tool lines in prose or content with a leading /, such as /@read: "file.ts" or /@server_tool: {"key":"value"}.
- Escape a literal etxt or EOF line in prose or content as /etxt or /EOF.
- If you are discussing, explaining, or showing the tool syntax itself, always escape those tool lines and etxt/EOF markers.
=== Using tools and closing
- After tool mode begins, every top-level non-empty line must start with @, unless it is body content for the active block or the etxt/EOF line closing @edit/@write/@todo.
- Use otxt/ntxt/etxt as the primary edit/write protocol.
- Use etxt as the primary closer for @edit, @write, and @todo. EOF is still accepted for compatibility, but etxt is preferred.
- Use otxt[1-3]: when you want a line range, or otxt: with no range.
- Use ntxt: for replacement text.
- otxt/ntxt blocks may replace a full block or just a substring within a line. You do not need to include the entire old line when the target text is unambiguous.
- After etxt or EOF, either start another top-level @tool line or end the response immediately.
- If the next top-level non-empty line is not @..., end the response instead of writing prose.
- If etxt/EOF is missing, the block may still close at the next top-level tool line or end of response as parser fallback, but you must not rely on that fallback.
=== Edit & Write stripping
- Successful @edit calls may later appear in conversation history as "Content placed in paired result below" placeholders.
- Successful @write calls may later appear as truncated previews ending with "..... see result for rest of write".
- Those placeholders do not mean the tool did nothing; the canonical applied edit blocks or post-write file snapshot are stored in the paired tool result.
- When history shows either form, treat the paired tool result as the source of truth for what changed.
=== Batching
Batching: multiple tool calls allowed.

Rules:
- Only use the tools listed here.
- Only the tool names listed here are valid top-level tool names.
- Do not invent syntax.
- Do not add unsupported values not shown in the syntax, and do not invent --flags; only use the argument shapes shown here. The only tool that flags are allowed in is the @bash tool.
- Do not mix prose with tool calls in the same response.
- Do not include prose after tool calls.
- The only thing allowed after a tool call, is another tool call or the end of the response.
- If you include prose after a tool call, the response will be rejected.
- @grep is optimized for code search, not raw grep. If the target may be in .txt, .md, docs, assets, generated files, or similar non-code content, add an explicit include= glob that matches those files.
- Read before edit unless the exact target and/or context is already in recent tool results.
- Batch independent calls only.
- Do not batch dependent calls. Such as editing a file in the same turn as reading but that read isnt even in your context yet, so you're assuming what the contents are.
- Do not batch speculative edits with discovery.
- Stop using tools when the task is complete.
===

Flow:
- Map -> @ls or @find
- Search -> @grep (grep is your most important tool for discovery and likely will get you to where you need to be 99% of the time)
- Read -> @read (prefer specific line ranges over full file reads)
- Change or identify -> Identify/deduce problem, @edit, or @write the solution
